name: Flood Street Residence — Flooded Interior
description: Three feet of grey silt water fills the ground floor of this shotgun house; the canvas kayak holds its position against the doorframe between the front room and the hall, and the deed box sits on the floor beneath a plaster block that has been shifting since the wet collapse two days ago.
atmosphericDetails: The silt smell is thickening as the water level drops — sweet and chemical underneath the river mud, a specific compound that experienced flood workers would recognize immediately
The silt line on the baseboard has dropped four inches since midmorning; the suspended particulate is consolidating, which means the box's lower seam is almost certainly packed

The ceiling above the left quadrant is stable at center and failing at its northwest corner; a plaster block the size of a dictionary, saturated weight approximately fifty-five pounds, sits directly over the box's left seam
The light inside is the color of river water filtered through a dirty window — flat, brownish, directionless
The 1978 lath work is visible where the plaster has already released from the northeast corner; the doubled keys are holding, the single keys are not
